conjecture_title: Open problem: Woodall's conjecture for fixed s
paper_title: Disproof of a Conjecture by Woodall
summary: The specific regime of fixed $s$ and $t \to \infty$ for Woodall's conjecture (that every $K_{s,t}$-minor-free graph is $(s+t-1)$-choosable) remains open as of 2026. Steiner's 2022 disproof (arXiv:2201.09115) only applies when $s$ and $t$ are of comparable size ($s \le t \le Cs$), and a 2025 follow-up by Vanderbush (Graphs Combin. 41, 2025) improves the disproof to much smaller comparable-size parameters (e.g., $t \ge 48$ for the $(t,t)$ case), but still entirely within the comparable-size regime. No analogous list-chromatic result to Kostochka's chromatic-number theorem is known for fixed $s$ with $t \to \infty$.
search_queries: Woodall conjecture Ks,t minor list chromatic choosable fixed s large t
Steiner 2201.09115 Woodall conjecture disproof Ks,t minor list coloring follow-up
"New Counterexamples" Woodall conjecture graph minors list coloring arxiv 2024 2025
Vanderbush "Woodall" counterexamples Ks,t minor list coloring choosable arxiv
notes: No follow-up resolving the fixed-$s$ case was found. A related follow-up, Vanderbush, 'New Counterexamples to a Conjecture by Woodall on Graph Minors and List Coloring', Graphs and Combinatorics 41, 103 (2025), DOI:10.1007/s00373-025-02957-y, improves Steiner's counterexample parameters (e.g., provides explicit counterexamples for $(s,t)=(t,t)$ with $t \ge 48$, $t \ne 49$, and $(s,t)=(t,t+1)$ with $t \ge 54$, $t \ne 55$) but remains in the comparable-size regime and does not address fixed $s$ with $t \to \infty$. The URL for this Springer paper could not be fully verified via WebFetch due to paywall redirection, so it is excluded from since_posted. The Fischer–Steiner paper arXiv:2304.04246 (Combin. Probab. Comput. 33, 2024) on choosability of $H$-minor-free graphs also does not address the fixed-$s$ regime of Woodall's conjecture.
